behaviors.description: Primary translator output is grammatically coherent and meaning-preserving (not literal word substitution).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is not local parsing logic; it is translation quality from the model. REAL-INFRA: Real network/API may be used, but the core boundary is model judgment quality, not transport mechanics. MOCK-HIDE: A stubbed translator would always return curated good text and hide bad fluency/meaning drift. LLM-DEP: Yes—correctness depends on real model output quality and can vary across runs/prompts/model versions. MULTI-STEP: No, single model decision, so not Workflow. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Low(Unit): call quality scorer on fixed strings; catches scorer bug, misses real model mistranslation. Current(Agentic): send representative multilingual sentences to real NMT API, assert adequacy/fluency thresholds (human-labeled set or semantic similarity+grammar checks); catches real semantic drift and literal translation artifacts; misses multi-stage orchestration bugs. High(Workflow): planner->translator->critic pipeline; catches cross-step regressions, misses nothing extra for this single-step behavior. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Workflow because behavior text only describes one translation decision, not multiple agentic handoffs.
behaviors.test_description: Run an agentic evaluation suite against the real translation API using a fixed bilingual gold set with idioms/context-dependent phrases; assert minimum adequacy/fluency score and meaning preservation rate. Fail if coherent-meaningful translation quality drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: On API 503, service transparently falls back to dictionary translator without returning caller error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is at module handoff (API client error path -> fallback translator -> response assembly). REAL-INFRA: Real OS/network not required to reproduce; in-process stub returning 503 exercises same branch. MOCK-HIDE: Full mocking of internal boundaries could hide wiring bugs, but a module-level integration test with stubbed API and real fallback stack catches them. LLM-DEP: No model-output-quality assertion; only error-path orchestration. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agentic chain. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Low(Unit): test fallback decision function with error code input; catches branch bug, misses wiring/DI issues. Current(Integration): wire service + API client stub (503) + real fallback translator/repo, assert successful translated response and no surfaced error; catches broken fallback plumbing and error mapping; misses real HTTP timeout/socket behavior. High(System): real HTTP server returning 503; catches transport misconfig, but not required for stated failure mode. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network/subprocess needed? No. (b) real AI/ML output needed? No. (c) multiple agentic steps? No. (d) LLM-governed handoff? No. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System due behavior text focusing on 'returns a 503 error' and transparent fallback semantics, which are reproducible with an in-process 503 stub.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with service wiring, API client test-double that returns 503, and real SQLite-backed fallback translator. Assert caller gets a translation payload (not error), and response indicates normal success contract.

behaviors.description: Dictionary fallback returns correct pre-stored translation for phrases present in SQLite.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Real failure usually appears at repo/query/schema boundary (phrase normalization, SQL query, collation), not just pure function logic. REAL-INFRA: Needs local DB interaction (SQLite) but not external OS/network beyond normal local file DB, fitting Integration. MOCK-HIDE: Mock repo can hide SQL mistakes, transaction/config issues, or incorrect table/column mapping. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Low(Unit): test map lookup function with in-memory dictionary; catches key-lookup logic bug, misses SQL/schema bugs. Current(Integration): seed test SQLite DB with phrase rows, invoke fallback translator end-to-end in-process, assert exact stored translation returned; catches query and mapping defects; misses filesystem locking edge cases. High(System): run against real on-disk DB with OS-level contention; catches lock/contention behavior not required by behavior text. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) OS/network/subprocess required? No. (b) AI output required? No. (c) multi-agentic chain? No. (d) LLM-governed orchestration? No. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected System because behavior explicitly targets correctness of pre-stored phrase retrieval, which is covered by local module+SQLite integration.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test using a seeded SQLite test database: request known phrase through fallback translator path and assert returned translation exactly matches stored value, including expected normalization rules.

behaviors.description: Quality tracking computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Behavior spans algorithm + persistence handoff; pure unit only covers math, not storage/wiring. REAL-INFRA: No real network/subprocess needed; local DB/storage integration sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: Mocked metric store may hide serialization/type/column bugs. LLM-DEP: No dependence on model quality; use fixed strings. MULTI-STEP: No. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Low(Unit): test edit-distance function with known string pairs; catches algorithm bug, misses persistence failures. Current(Integration): invoke comparison service with fixed primary/fallback strings and real metrics repository (SQLite), assert stored numeric distance equals expected value; catches compute->store contract bugs; misses OS-level DB corruption/locking. High(System): include real external metrics sink/process; catches infra outages not required by stated behavior. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) OS/network/subprocess required? No. (b) AI output required? No. (c) multi-agentic chain? No. (d) LLM handoff orchestration? No. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Agentic/System because behavior text is deterministic computation+storage ('computes edit distance' and 'stores metric'), not model judgment or external infra dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with deterministic primary/fallback strings (e.g., 'color' vs 'colour'), run quality tracker, then read persisted metric row and assert exact edit distance and linkage to request/translation IDs.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, service returns primary translation and logs quality comparison asynchronously.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Failure is orchestration across modules (primary result selection + background metric task dispatch/persist). REAL-INFRA: Can be reproduced in-process with controlled async executor; no real network/OS primitive required for stated behavior. MOCK-HIDE: Over-mocking may hide race/order issues; integration with real scheduler/queue component and metrics store catches them. LLM-DEP: Execution touches primary model call path, but behavior asserts routing/asynchrony only, not model output quality. MULTI-STEP: Not multiple model decisions; one call plus background bookkeeping. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: Low(Unit): test selector picks primary when both non-null; catches branch bug, misses async logging pipeline failures. Current(Integration): wire service with deterministic translator stubs for both paths, real async job runner component, real metrics repo; assert response equals primary output and metric log appears eventually; catches response-path blocking/regression and dropped background jobs; misses real network failures to external API. High(System): real NMT HTTP + real worker process; catches deployment/transport issues beyond behavior scope. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) real OS/network/subprocess needed? No for this behavior. (b) real AI/ML output needed? No, this behavior EXCLUSIVELY tests orchestration and background logging. (c) multi-agentic chain? No. (d) LLM-governed stage handoff? No, handoff rule is deterministic preference for primary. REJECTION-GATE: Rejected Agentic because behavior text does not require judging translation quality; it requires 'returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background,' which is infrastructure/orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with both translators returning deterministic strings, invoke service, assert immediate response equals primary translator output, then await and verify a persisted quality-comparison record was created asynchronously without blocking response.
